title: Corrections/erratum process (versioned, attributed)
context: The stats-for-clinicians project applies a zero-tolerance rule on stated statistical falsehoods: any released explainer found to contain a statistical falsehood (or a wrong 'correction') is a release-failing defect that must be addressed with a versioned erratum. A public corrections mechanism is also a mark of scientific integrity — it signals that the project takes accuracy seriously and creates a path for any expert to flag an error without it being suppressed. This M3 task follows release-019 and establishes the erratum process before wider adoption makes errors harder to correct quickly.
objective: Design and publish a corrections/erratum process that allows any reader to flag a statistical error, routes it to the biostatistician reviewer for verification, and produces a versioned, attributed erratum if confirmed — while superseding but not silently overwriting earlier versions.
acceptanceCriteria: A public mechanism exists for anyone to flag a potential statistical error or misleading framing (e.g. a GitHub issue template labeled 'Statistical Correction Request' with a structured form: claim location, suspected error, proposed correction, supporting source).

The process specifies: within a defined SLA (e.g. 30 days), the biostatistician reviewer assesses the flagged claim and renders a decision (confirmed error / not an error / needs further review); the decision is recorded publicly.

A confirmed falsehood triggers a versioned erratum committed to the project repo: the erratum records the original claim, the correction, the source supporting the correction, the reviewer who confirmed it, and the date; the original version is marked as superseded (not silently overwritten).
A confirmed falsehood in one explainer triggers a review of related explainers for the same error pattern; the scope of that review is recorded.
Superseded versions remain accessible (e.g. via a git tag) and are clearly marked as superseded in any display; adopters who pinned a version are made aware via the erratum.
The erratum process is committed to the project repo (e.g. CORRECTIONS.md or equivalent) and referenced from the project README and from each explainer's review block.
The maintainer and biostatistician reviewer have approved the process document.
resources: tasks/stats-for-clinicians-release-019.json — release v1.0 (prerequisite: something to issue errata against)
PLAN.md — Sustainability & maintenance (corrections/errata section); Quality gates (zero-falsehood rule)
output: A committed corrections/erratum process document (e.g. CORRECTIONS.md), a public error-flagging mechanism (e.g. issue template), and a documented workflow for biostatistician review, versioned erratum publication, and related-explainer review. Approved by the maintainer and biostatistician reviewer.
